sanskrit: स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या जगत् प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च। रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्गाः॥
transliteration: sthāne hṛṣīkeśa tava prakīrtyā jagat prahṛṣyaty anurajyate ca | rakṣāṃsi bhītāni diśo dravanti sarve namasyanti ca siddha-saṅghāḥ
literal_meaning: Rightly, O Lord of the senses, through Your renown the world rejoices and becomes attached; the demons, stricken with fear, flee in all directions; all hosts of perfected beings bow down.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s psyche has moved from horror to wholehearted acceptance: the same cosmic display that terrified him now evokes awe-based trust. He recognizes that the Divine’s destructive aspect is not cruelty but necessary order, integrating fear into reverence.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic integration: the split between warrior duty and compassionate self is reconciled through transpersonal trust; fear is metabolized into purposeful surrender.
trigger_scenario: A whistle-blower who feared public shaming sees their revelation spark global reform; critics flee while respected leaders salute, and the whistle-blower realizes the upheaval was morally necessary.
anchor_text: See divine order in chaos and bow in trust.
